_info_step1.text: Step 1: Open the Bosch login URL in your browser

_login_instructions.text: How it works: click the blue button below to open the Bosch SingleKey login in a new browser tab. Sign in (with captcha / MFA if Bosch asks). Bosch then redirects your browser to https://www.bosch.com/boschcam?code=XXX&state=YYY — the page usually shows a blank screen or a 404, that is expected. Copy the FULL URL from the browser's address bar and paste it into Step 2 below.

_login_urgency_warning.text: ⚠ Copy the redirect URL within ~60 seconds. The Bosch auth code in that URL expires very quickly — if you wait too long, the paste will fail with 'code expired'. Keep this dialog open in a second tab so you can paste immediately. If it expires, just click 'Open Bosch Login' again for a fresh URL.

confirm.text: This clears all Bosch tokens and restarts the adapter. You will need to log in again. Continue?
confirm.title: Reset login?

redirect_url.label: Pasted callback URL (https://www.bosch.com/boschcam?code=...)
redirect_url.help: Paste the FULL URL from the browser address bar immediately after Bosch redirects you — within ~60 seconds, otherwise the auth code expires. The 404 page or blank screen you may see is expected; only the URL matters.

_rtsp_help.text: By default the per-camera RTSP proxy listens on 127.0.0.1 only — reachable from the ioBroker host itself (cameras adapter, FFmpeg, go2rtc). Enable LAN exposure to let a recorder on a different host pull the stream. The published cameras.<id>.stream_url already carries Digest credentials, so the URL works as-is once pasted into the recorder.

rtsp_expose_to_lan.label: Expose RTSP proxy to LAN
rtsp_expose_to_lan.help: Bind the proxy to 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) instead of 127.0.0.1. Required for BlueIris / Frigate on a separate host.

rtsp_external_host.label: External hostname / LAN IP
rtsp_external_host.help: Hostname or IP the recorder should use to reach the proxy, e.g. "192.168.1.50". Used in the published stream_url. Leave empty to keep 127.0.0.1.

stream_max_session_duration.label: Max session duration (s, 0 = camera default)
stream_max_session_duration.help: The RTSP maxSessionDuration written into the published stream URL. The camera defaults to 3600 s (60 min). A continuous go2rtc / recorder pull can drop with a timeout at that boundary if the session expires before the adapter's renewed session takes over. Raise this (e.g. 5000) to keep the stream running longer between renewals. Range 600-21600 s; 0 keeps the camera-reported value.

_persistent_endpoint_header.text: Always-on RTSP endpoint for external recorders

stream_persistent_endpoint.label: Keep the RTSP endpoint reachable at all times
stream_persistent_endpoint.help: OFF (default): the local RTSP proxy only listens while a live stream is running, so a recorder such as iobroker.cameras, BlueIris or Frigate that polls cameras.<id>.stream_url on its own schedule gets 'Connection refused' whenever the stream is off. ON: the adapter keeps a small listener bound on a stable per-camera port all the time. The Bosch session is opened automatically the moment a recorder connects and released again after the idle timeout below, so the endpoint is always reachable without permanently occupying one of the 3 shared Bosch sessions. Recommended when you integrate the camera into iobroker.cameras. Forum #84538.

stream_persistent_idle_timeout.label: Release session after idle (s)
stream_persistent_idle_timeout.help: Seconds with no recorder connected before the on-demand Bosch session is closed again. The listener stays reachable; only the camera session is released to free the shared Bosch session budget. A stream you explicitly turned on (livestream_enabled = true) is never closed by this timer. Range 10-3600 s. Default 60 s.

_events_help.text: When the camera reports a motion / person / audio_alarm event (via FCM push or polling fallback), automatically fetch a fresh snapshot and write a base64-encoded copy into cameras.<id>.last_event_image. Perfect for Telegram / Signal / Matrix push notifications: your automation reads last_event_image and forwards the JPEG directly, no separate snapshot_trigger needed. Bursts (continuous motion) reuse the warm Bosch session so quota stays low.

auto_snapshot_on_motion.label: Auto-snapshot on motion event
auto_snapshot_on_motion.help: Fetches a fresh JPEG on every motion / person / audio_alarm event and updates both cameras.<id>.snapshot_path (file store) and cameras.<id>.last_event_image (base64 string).

motion_active_window.label: Motion-active window (s)
motion_active_window.help: How long cameras.<id>.motion_active stays true after the last motion event before auto-clearing. Range: 10–300 s. Default: 90 s.

_mjpeg_help.text: Gen2 cameras (Eyes Outdoor II, Eyes Indoor II) expose a low-latency MJPEG stream on RTSP inst=3. When enabled and the camera is reachable on the LAN, snapshots are fetched via a local FFmpeg subprocess (~150-300 ms) instead of the cloud snap.jpg path (~500-1500 ms). Requires ffmpeg on the ioBroker host. Falls back to snap.jpg on any error.

use_mjpeg_snapshot.label: Use MJPEG inst=3 for Gen2 snapshots (fast LAN path)
use_mjpeg_snapshot.help: Requires ffmpeg on the ioBroker host. Automatically falls back to snap.jpg on errors.

snapshot_http_port.label: Snapshot HTTP server port (0 = off)
snapshot_http_port.help: When > 0, serves the latest JPEG per camera at http://<host>:<port>/<camId>.jpg (LAN, no auth) and publishes a snapshot_url state (role url.cam) for VIS / type-detector. Suggested: 8095.

_saving_help.text: Your cameras share a hard limit of only 3 simultaneous Bosch sessions across the Bosch app, Home Assistant, this adapter and any recorder. Opening a live stream or fetching a fresh snapshot consumes one of those sessions, and the cloud is polled regularly per camera. The options below let you reduce that load. On a fresh install the request-heavy options are OFF by default; turn them on only if you need them. Motion detection, manual snapshots and live streams (started on demand via cameras.<id>.livestream_enabled) keep working regardless.

startup_snapshot.label: Fetch a snapshot for every camera on adapter start
startup_snapshot.help: OFF (recommended): when the adapter starts, each camera's online/offline state is determined the cheap, session-less way (a LAN TCP ping, falling back to the cloud /ping and /commissioned checks) — no Bosch stream session is opened. ON: a real snapshot is fetched per camera at start, which gives an immediate boot image but opens one of the 3 shared Bosch sessions per camera every time the adapter (re)starts.

poll_interval.label: Cloud poll interval (s)
poll_interval.help: How often the adapter polls the Bosch cloud per camera for status, motion config, light, events and similar. Each tick is several cloud requests per camera, so raising this value reduces request volume roughly proportionally — e.g. 120 s halves it versus the 60 s default. The trade-off is that changes made in the Bosch app appear in ioBroker a bit later. Motion push (FCM) is unaffected and stays near-instant. Range 30-3600 s. Default 60 s (unchanged behaviour).

enable_diagnostics_polling.label: Poll diagnostic datapoints
enable_diagnostics_polling.help: ON (default): periodically refresh the rarely-changing diagnostic datapoints — motion zones, light/ambient config, alarm settings, ONVIF/RCP info and cloud feature flags. Turn OFF to stop these cloud reads entirely if you don't use those datapoints; the core states (online, privacy, motion, snapshots, light, livestream) keep working.

poll_interval_slow.label: Diagnostic poll interval (s)
poll_interval_slow.help: How often the diagnostic datapoints above are refreshed (separately from the main poll interval). Higher = fewer cloud requests for data that barely changes. Range 60-7200 s. Default 300 s.

stream_idle_reaper.label: Turn off a live stream when nobody is watching
stream_idle_reaper.help: OFF (default): once you enable a live stream (cameras.<id>.livestream_enabled = true) it stays connected and keeps occupying one of the 3 shared Bosch sessions until you turn it off. ON (experimental): the adapter watches how many clients (go2rtc, a recorder, VLC) are actually pulling the local RTSP proxy; if none have been pulling for the timeout below, it turns the live stream off automatically to free the session. A stream that something is really watching is never stopped. It does not auto-restart — the next consumer or a manual toggle starts it again.

stream_idle_timeout.label: Idle timeout (s)
stream_idle_timeout.help: How many seconds with zero clients pulling the stream before it is turned off. Range 30-3600 s. Default 180 s.

_info_mqtt.text: Publish camera events to an MQTT broker

_mqtt_help.text: When enabled, every motion / person / audio_alarm event is published as a JSON payload to <prefix>/<cam_id>/motion|person|audio. Compatible with Node-RED, openHAB, Home Assistant, Frigate, and any other MQTT consumer.

mqtt_enabled.label: Enable MQTT Bridge
mqtt_enabled.help: Connect to the MQTT broker below and publish camera events.

mqtt_broker_host.label: Broker host / IP
mqtt_broker_host.help: Hostname or IP of your MQTT broker, e.g. 192.168.1.10 or mqtt.example.com.

mqtt_tls.label: Use TLS (mqtts://)
mqtt_tls.help: Enable encrypted connection. Requires a TLS-capable broker.

mqtt_password.label: Password (optional)
mqtt_password.help: Stored encrypted in the ioBroker object store.

mqtt_topic_prefix.label: Topic prefix
mqtt_topic_prefix.help: All events are published under <prefix>/<cam_id>/motion|person|audio. Default: bosch/cameras.
